dawu.usage: Primary brand color. Dawn ember — the color of Nairobi's horizon at 5:50am. Use on CTAs, bold headlines, and wherever energy needs anchoring.

usiku.usage: Primary dark background. Pre-dawn indigo-black — the sky before the run begins. Default background for event posters, dark-mode surfaces, and night photography overlays.

haraka.usage: Secondary energetic coral-red. The pulse — heartbeats, dance floor heat, the moment you cross the finish line. Use for notifications, urgency states, and high-energy moments.

jua.usage: Warm solar gold. The sun fully cresting the horizon at 7:15am. Use for highlights, celebration moments, star elements, and warmth accents layered over photography.

pumzi.usage: Warm cream — primary text-on-dark and light background base. Named for breath: the first deep one at the starting line. Never use pure white; use Pumzi instead.

ngozi.usage: Warm sandy brown — Nairobi laterite earth, skin-adjacent warmth. Use as muted text on dark backgrounds, dividers, secondary surface details, and mid-tone photography overlays.

baridi.usage: Deep cool blue-grey. The morning air before the sun's warmth hits. Use as secondary dark surface, card backgrounds on dark-mode screens, and as a dark background alternative to Usiku.

volt.usage: Electric chartreuse. The accent that cuts through everything — photography, dark backgrounds, light backgrounds. Use sparingly and with intention: wristbands, one word in a headline, key CTAs, the dot after MOZAKA.

dawn_poster.headline: usiku
dawn_poster.note: Morning / bright-mode combination. Warm cream ground with dark headline and ember CTA.

night_poster.headline: pumzi
night_poster.note: Event poster default. Deep night sky, cream text, volt for a single high-energy element.

energy_burst.headline: pumzi
energy_burst.note: Use for finish-line posts and high-celebration moments. Hot, warm, proud.

data_card.headline: pumzi
data_card.note: Run stats, pace cards, leaderboard. Cool dark surface reads technical.
